title: Vitellariopsis marginata
taxonomy: Vitellariopsis marginata is classified in the family Sapotaceae. This classification places it within the order Ebenales and the clade Asterids. The plant belongs to the genus Vitellariopsis, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. Vitellariopsis marginata is a species of tree that is closely related to other species within the same genus. Its classification is part of a broader understanding of the plant's evolutionary history and relationships within the plant kingdom.
uses: Vitellariopsis marginata, also known as the yellow milkwood or the ivory nut, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its bark, leaves, and seeds are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The seeds are also said to have antifungal properties and are used to treat fungal infections. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Vitellariopsis marginata in treating these conditions.

intro: Vitellariopsis marginata, commonly known as the African plum or bush plum, is a deciduous tree native to tropical Africa.
